bids.reasoning: It is the very beginning of the negotiation (t=0.0) and the opponent has opened with an offer that yields my lowest possible utility on all issues. I will counter by asserting my ideal preferences to establish a strong starting position, proposing the maximum utility for myself across the board.

The negotiation has just started (t=0.02), and the opponent has repeated their opening offer which provides me with minimum utility (0.20). I will show a slight willingness to compromise by conceding marginally on my least important issue (issueD) while maintaining my most preferred values for all other issues, aiming for an initial high utility of 0.973.

The opponent is holding entirely firm on their opening offer of all 'valueD'. Because it is very early in the negotiation (t=0.04), I will continue to hold firm on my highest-weight issues (issueA, issueB, and issueC) while making a minor concession on my lowest-weight issue (issueD) to signal a willingness to move.

The negotiation is still in the very early stages (t=0.06). The opponent has rigidly repeated their opening offer which serves only their utility and provides me with the lowest possible outcome. Since I still have ample time, I will maintain my positions on my highest-weight issues (issueA and issueB) to protect my core interests, while presenting a minor concession on my lower-weight issues (issueC and issueD) to encourage reciprocal movement.

The opponent is holding entirely firm on all issues, continually bidding their likely ideal values (valueD for everything). Since we are still early in the negotiation, I will concede fully on my lowest-priority issue, issueD, in hopes of breaking the deadlock and prompting them to reciprocate. I will hold firm on my highest-priority issues to maintain a high utility (0.92).

The opponent is holding firm on their opening offer of all 'valueD' without making any concessions. Since it is still early in the negotiation (t=0.10), I need to remain patient and make small concessions to see if I can coax them into movement. I will maintain my top values for my most critical issues (issueA and issueB), while offering modest concessions on my lower-weighted issues (issueC and issueD).

The negotiation is still early (t=0.12), so time pressure is very low. However, the opponent is holding entirely firm on an offer that yields minimal utility for me, showing no willingness to concede yet. I will maintain my highest utility requests on my most critical issues (issueA and issueB), while offering another minor permutation on issueC and issueD to signal continued willingness to explore combinations.

The opponent is holding entirely firm on their initial offer, which gives me my minimum possible utility (0.2). As it is still early in the negotiation (t=0.14), I will avoid making large concessions but continue to explore the search space. I will maintain my highest preferred values on the most important issue (issueA) while probing if the opponent prefers a concession on issueB (valueB) instead of issueC or issueD.

The opponent is holding firm on their opening offer which provides me with minimum utility (0.2). It is still quite early in the negotiation (t=0.16), so I do not need to make drastic concessions yet. I will continue probing the opponent by making a small concession on issueD while maintaining high-value positions on my most heavily weighted issues (issueA and issueB). This targeted concession aims to discover the opponent's priorities without conceding too much utility.

The opponent is repeating their lowest possible offer for my utility (valueD for all issues), refusing to concede so far. As the time fraction is still early (t=0.18), I will maintain a firm stance on my highest importance issue (issueA) and make small, incremental concessions on lower priority issues like issueB and issueD to see if they engage.

The opponent is holding entirely firm on their opening offer across all issues despite my gradual concessions. At t=0.20, time pressure is still low, so I will maintain my top value for my most important issue (issueA) while continuing to explore compromises by marginally softening my stance on the lower-weighted issues. I will offer valueB across issues B, C, and D to test if the opponent will reciprocate any movement.

The opponent continues to hold firm on their opening offer of all `valueD`, refusing to concede on any issue. Since it is still early in the negotiation (t=0.22), I will maintain strong positions on my most heavily weighted issues while making targeted concessions on the less critical issues. I am now offering `valueD` for `issueD` to see if granting them their requested value on my lowest-priority issue encourages them to move on the others.

The opponent is stonewalling, consistently offering their presumed most-preferred outcome which grants me minimum utility. Since it is still early in the negotiation (t=0.24), I am not pressured to concede heavily. I will propose an offer that holds firm on the most important issue (issueA) while continuing to explore small, incremental concessions on the remaining issues to find a mutually acceptable tradeoff.

The opponent has rigidly repeatedly offered the same bid proposing the lowest possible utility for me on all issues (`valueD` across the board). Since the time fraction is still early at 0.26, I will continue a slow pace of concessions. I am holding firm on `issueA` (my most important issue) while systematically making minor concessions on lower-weight issues like `issueC` and `issueD` to explore whether the opponent will eventually deviate from their opening offer.

The opponent is holding entirely firm on their starting offer of valueD for all issues, which provides me with only the reservation value. Since the time fraction is still relatively early (t=0.28), I will slowly concede on the less critical issues (issueC and issueD) while maintaining my highest utility value on my most important issue (issueA) in order to encourage the opponent to finally make a concession.

The opponent is holding entirely firm on their opening offer of all 'valueD', which provides me with my lowest possible utility of 0.20. As the time fraction is still fairly early (t=0.3), I am not pressured to collapse to their level, but I will slowly discover tradeoffs by dropping on my less critical issues. By offering valueC for my least important issues (issueC and issueD) while keeping my core issues (issueA and issueB) at high utility values (valueA and valueB), I project a utility of 0.76 for myself while seeking any traction from the opponent.

The opponent has remained completely inflexible, repeating the exact same offer since the beginning of the negotiation, which yields my minimum possible utility. At this stage (t=0.32), I will continue a slow concession strategy by dropping my request on issueD to their preferred value (valueD) and issueC to valueC. This maintains strong positions on my most heavily weighted issues (issueA and issueB), while probing if the opponent is willing to meet me partway now that I have conceded on the lesser issues.

The opponent is holding extremely firm, repeating their initial offer of 'valueD' for every issue natively. Since my time fraction is 0.34, I will continue making measured concessions by experimenting with different value combinations to uncover the opponent's true priorities. In this turn, I will concede significantly on issueC to give them one of their requested values, while holding firm on my highest-priority issues (issueA and issueB) to maintain a strong overall utility.

The opponent has repeatedly sent the exact same, maximally self-serving offer (`valueD` for all issues) without making any concessions or sending messages. Given the time fraction is still only 0.36, I am under no immediate pressure to accept this uncompromising stance. I will continue probing for acceptable tradeoffs by exploring valueC in my second most important issue (issueB) while holding firm on issueA to maintain a utility of around 0.84.

The opponent has rigidly repeated the identical low-utility offer throughout the entire negotiation, signaling extreme inflexibility. Having held firm on my top priority (issueA) for multiple rounds, I will now test a concession on issueA while reclaiming maximum utility on issueB and issueC to see if shifting the axis of negotiation prompts a meaningful response, all while safely preserving an overall utility around 0.86.

The opponent is repeating their opening offer which provides me the lowest possible utility, indicating an unwillingness to negotiate so far. At t=0.40, I will continue to make small concessions, exploring different trade-offs while keeping my utility above 0.83. I will concede slightly on issueA and issueC to see if they prefer those issues.

My utility for the current best offer on the table is extremely low, as the opponent is holding completely firm on their initial bid of all valueDs. To break the deadlock and continue displaying a willingness to compromise, I will offer valueD on issueD (my lowest importance issue) while sacrificing a bit on issueA to see if this combination aligns better with their unstated priorities. This keeps my utility reasonably high (around 0.81) early in the mid-game.

The opponent has rigidly maintained the exact same offer (giving themselves maximum utility on all issues, presumably) in every single round since t=0.00. Since we are at t=0.44, I will continue to systematically test lower-utility concessions to find an area of compromise, reducing my target utility to ~0.78. I am conceding slightly on my more important issues (issueA and issueB) while maintaining high value on issueC to see if this profile better aligns with their preferences.

The opponent is holding absolutely firm by repeating the exact same offer (valueD for all issues) throughout the entire negotiation, despite my continued concessions. Since we are approaching the midway point (t=0.46), I will make a measured concession on my lower-weight issues like issueC and issueD, including giving them their preferred 'valueD' on issueD. This keeps my utility at a reasonable 0.758 while signaling flexibility.

The opponent has rigidly maintained their initial offer of `valueD` for all issues without any concessions. As we are nearing the halfway point (t=0.48), I will systematically test offers that grant them their preferred `valueD` on my least important issues (like issueD and partially issueC) while retaining moderate to high utility values on my most important issues (issueA and issueB). This aims to signal flexibility without unnecessarily sacrificing too much utility.

We are at the halfway mark (t=0.5) and the opponent has stubbornly repeated the exact same offer (all valueD) every round without conceding or sending any messages. I will continue to protect my highest-weight issues (issueA and issueB), maintaining valueA and valueB respectively, but I will fully concede on my lowest-weight issues (issueC and issueD) to valueD to test if the opponent is willing to accept a highly asymmetric compromise that gives them what they want on half the issues.

The opponent has stubbornly bid the exact same values {valueD, valueD, valueD, valueD} for every single offer since the start, showing no willingness to negotiate. Since we are at the halfway point, I will concede fully on my two least important issues (issueC and issueD, granting them valueD) while holding firm on my most important issues (issueA and issueB) to target a utility of 0.76. This tests whether they are willing to accept a partial concession or if they will deadlock the negotiation.

The opponent has stubbornly held to demanding `valueD` for all issues throughout the entire first half of the negotiation, showing no signs of compromise. Since we are just past the midpoint (t=0.54), I will continue to hold firm on my most important issue (`issueA`) while offering further concessions on my lower-weighted issues (such as accepting `valueD` for `issueD` and stepping down to `valueB` or `valueC` on mid-tier issues) in hopes of breaking the deadlock without dropping my utility below an acceptable threshold (~0.70).

The opponent has stubbornly bid valueD for all issues from the start, refusing to make any concessions. To break this deadlock as we pass the halfway point, I will concede fully to their favored valueD on issues C and D, where my utility weights are lowest (0.2 and 0.1). Meanwhile, I'll protect my high-priority issues (A and B) by offering valueB and valueA, keeping my utility around 0.65 while hopefully encouraging reciprocity.

The opponent has finally shown a slight concession on `issueA` at t=0.58 (moving from `valueD` to `valueC`). Since `issueA` is my most heavily weighted issue, I want to capitalize on this flexibility while continuing to concede on my lower-weighted issues (such as `issueD`, where I am granting their assumed preferred choice `valueD`). My offer targets a utility around 0.68, demonstrating a willingness to reach a middle ground in the mid-game phase.

At t=0.60, the opponent has stubbornly stuck to 'valueD' across all issues, only briefly wavering to 'valueC' on issueA. This suggests issueA may be their least important issue, which aligns perfectly with my highest-weighted issue. I will firmly target 'valueA' for issueA to secure my most critical utility, while making meaningful concessions on the lower-weight issues like issueC and issueD to inch closer to a mutually acceptable agreement.

The opponent remains resolute in demanding 'valueD' across all issues, flashing a single concession on 'issueA' (valueC at t=0.58) before immediately reverting. This signals 'issueA' is relatively cheap for them to concede on. I will continue to claim 'valueA' on my highest-priority issue (issueA) while offering further concessions on my less critical issues (issueC and issueD) to find a middle ground as we progress toward the deadline.

The opponent generally demands valueD across all issues, though they have recently shown a slight willingness to concede on issueA by offering valueC. Given that we are entering the slightly later stages of negotiation (t=0.64) and the opponent is extremely stubborn, I will yield completely on my lowest importance issues (issueC and issueD) by offering their preferred valueD. I will simultaneously protect my margins on my highly weighted issues (issueA and issueB) by offering valueB, maintaining a reasonable utility while offering a sensible compromise.

The time fraction is 0.66, entering the later stages of the negotiation. The opponent continues to strongly request valueD on almost all issues, though they occasionally show slight flexibility on issueA by proposing valueC. To signal cooperation, I will concede entirely to their preference on issues C and D, where my weights are the lowest, and offer a moderate value on issueB, while mostly holding firm on issueA to maintain a reasonable utility above 0.60.

The opponent has just conceded on issueA to valueB, which provides me good utility since it's my most important issue. By responding with a bid that mutually agrees on issueA (valueB), issueC (valueD), and issueD (valueD), I am sending a strong signal that we are close to a deal. We now only differ on issueB, where I will propose valueB to maintain a utility of around 0.57, giving us room to find a final compromise before the deadline.

The opponent has shown consistent resistance on issues B, C, and D, stubbornly offering valueD, but has recently shown willingness to concede on issueA by offering valueB and valueC. Since issueA is my most important issue (weight 0.4), I will gladly anchor there at valueB. I will concede entirely on issueD (my lowest weight) and move towards their preference on issueC, while retaining reasonable value on issueB to maintain a utility above 0.6.

The opponent continues to hold firm on valueD for issues B, C, and D, but has demonstrated a willingness to concede on issueA (reaching valueB at t=0.68). Since issueA is my most heavily weighted issue (0.4) and B is my second (0.3), this indicates a clear path to an optimal tradeoff: I can make concessions on issues C and D in exchange for maximizing my gains on issues A and B. I will offer a combination that secures my highest utility values for A and B while yielding ground to valueC and valueD for the opponent's preferred issues.

The negotiation is entering its final quarter (t=0.74). The opponent has rigidly insisted on their valueD for all issues, only briefly exploring valueC and valueB for issueA before reverting to all D's. I will protect my top-priority issueA at valueA while making a further concession down to valueC on issueB and matching their valueD on issueC and issueD, resulting in an offer utility around 0.60, to see if they will break their pattern on issueA and issueB.

The opponent has rigidly held issues C and D at 'valueD', but at `t=0.68` they conceded issue A to 'valueB' and just now at `t=0.76` they conceded issue B to 'valueC'. By combining their historical concessions into a single offer, I aim to present a highly acceptable package that still nets me roughly 0.49 utility, safely above my reservation value as the deadline draws nearer.

The negotiation is nearing the late stages (t=0.78), and the opponent has shown minor willingness to concede on my highly valued issues (A and B) but heavily insists on valueD for issues C and D. To break the deadlock and secure a mutually agreeable compromise before time runs out, I will concede primarily on issueC and issueD which have the lowest weights for me. I am pairing these concessions with moderately high utility outcomes for my most important issues to secure a reasonable total utility just near 0.6.

The opponent has signaled a willingness to concede on my most important issues over time, proposing `valueB` for `issueA` at t=0.68 and `valueB` for `issueB` at t=0.80. Given that we are entering the late stages of negotiation (t=0.8), I will combine these independent concessions into a single package. I am fully conceding to their preferred `valueD` on my lowest-weight issues (`issueC` and `issueD`) to secure `valueB` on my top two priorities, representing a solid compromise utility of 0.571.

The current time fraction is 0.82, so we are in the late game. The opponent has started making simultaneous concessions on issueA (valueC) and issueB (valueC), showing willingness to compromise since they previously accepted valueB on both individually. I will offer a compromise that asks for valueB on my high-priority issues (issueA and issueB), valueC on issueD, and gives them their preferred valueD on issueC, maintaining a utility of ~0.60.

The negotiation is entering the late stage (t=0.84), and the opponent has started to concede slightly on issueA and issueB (offering valueC and valueB at times), while remaining completely rigid on issueC and issueD (demanding valueD). I will accommodate their strong preference for valueD on issueC and issueD, which are my lowest-weighted issues, while holding reasonably firm on my heavily weighted issues (issueA and issueB) by demanding valueB for both. This yields a utility around 0.62 for me and hopefully meets the opponent in the middle.

The opponent has significantly conceded on `issueB` (offering `valueB`) and `issueA` (offering `valueC`), while strictly holding `issueC` and `issueD` at `valueD`. Since time is at 0.86, I will accept their rigid stance on `issueC` and `issueD`, and partially accept their concession on `issueB` but ask for `valueA` to offset the utility loss, while offering `valueB` on `issueA`. This ensures my utility remains relatively strong (~0.65) while attempting to find a workable combination.

The current time fraction is 0.88, indicating we are in the late stages of negotiation. The opponent has firmly entrenched themselves on `issueC: valueD` and `issueD: valueD`, but has shown willingness to concede occasionally on `issueA` (offering `valueB` at t=0.88) and `issueB` (offering `valueB` at t=0.86). I will therefore fully concede `issueC` and `issueD` to their preferred `valueD` while meeting them at their most generous prior concessions for my two most crucial issues (`issueA` and `issueB`).
